topic: Loading and parsing JSON data in p5.js using asynchronous functions and callbacks.

concepts.name: JSON (JavaScript Object Notation)
concepts.explanation: A data format consisting of an array containing objects, where each object uses key-value pairs (like 'city': 'New York') separated by colons and commas.
concepts.why_it_matters: It is the standard way to structure data so that a program can easily read specific properties like latitude or population.

concepts.name: loadJSON()
concepts.explanation: A p5.js function used to fetch a JSON file from a path and turn it into a usable JavaScript object.
concepts.why_it_matters: It allows your sketch to use external datasets rather than hard-coding every single value manually.

concepts.name: preload()
concepts.explanation: A special p5.js function that runs before setup() and draw(), ensuring all external assets (like data files) are fully loaded before the sketch starts.
concepts.why_it_matters: If you try to use data in setup() before it has finished loading, your code will crash because the variable is still empty.

concepts.name: Asynchronous Programming & Callbacks
concepts.explanation: The idea that the computer doesn't know exactly when a file will finish downloading. A 'callback' is a function you provide that tells the computer: 'Once you finally get this data, run this specific piece of code.'
concepts.why_it_matters: Without callbacks, a program might try to draw something using data that hasn't arrived yet because the network was slow.

concepts.name: Data Parsing/Preprocessing
concepts.explanation: Using a callback function to loop through complex, nested data (like an array of 'features') to find specific values or simplify the structure before using it in a sketch.
concepts.why_it_matters: Raw data is often messy or deeply nested; you need to 'clean' it or extract what you need so your drawing code stays simple.

code_examples.demonstrates: The basic way to load a local JSON file from an assets folder into a global variable.
code_examples.common_issue: Forgetting to use preload(), which results in 'cities' being undefined when setup() runs.

code_examples.demonstrates: Using a success callback (handleData) to process data immediately after it loads.
code_examples.common_issue: Passing a function that doesn't accept the 'data' parameter.

code_examples.demonstrates: Iterating through a nested GeoJSON structure to find the maximum value in a specific property.
code_examples.common_issue: Incorrectly accessing nested properties (e.g., forgetting that the value is inside '.properties').

mistakes_and_fixes.symptom: Data won't load or gives a 'CORS' error
mistakes_and_fixes.cause: Trying to load data directly from another website instead of uploading it locally.
mistakes_and_fixes.fix: Upload the JSON file to your project's assets folder or use a local server.

mistakes_and_fixes.symptom: The canvas appears but nothing is drawn/nothing shows up
mistakes_and_fixes.cause: The data might have loaded, but you haven't written the code to actually *use* the data in draw() or setup().
mistakes_and_fixes.fix: Use console.log(variableName) to check if the data actually exists in memory.

mistakes_and_fixes.symptom: Data contains weird characters or extra text (like footnotes)
mistakes_and_fixes.cause: The data was scraped from a website and includes non-data elements.
mistakes_and_fixes.fix: Manually clean the data or use string manipulation in your code to strip unwanted characters.

key_moments.description: Visual inspection of a raw JSON file to understand the structure (keys, values, arrays) before coding.
Explaining why preload() is essential for data: it guarantees the data exists before setup/draw run.
Explaining the necessity of success and error callbacks to prevent the computer from waiting forever on a bad connection.
Using console.log() to verify that the loaded JSON is actually present in the browser's memory.

leads_to: Mapping data to visual variables (size, color, position)
Working with APIs and real-time data streams
student_questions: How do I load a JSON file into my p5.js sketch?
Why is my data undefined even though I loaded it?
What's the difference between setup and preload?
How do I see what's inside my JSON file while coding?
Why can't I just load data from a website URL directly?
How do I use the values inside a JSON file to draw shapes?
What is a callback function in simple terms?
My code runs but nothing happens on the screen, how do I check if my data is there?
How do I loop through a list of cities from a JSON file?
What is CORS and why does it break my data loading?
module: Module 4 – Poetry of The World, Oceans of Data
